paper.title: Classical Be Stars and Classical Be Star Binaries from LAMOST DR12

extraction.summary: The paper identifies 37 runaway stars with total peculiar velocities ranging from ~40 to ~101 km/s using the classical runaway criterion (V_pec,tot > 40 km/s). However, the paper does not present any of these objects as possibly unbound from the Milky Way. No bound/unbound assessment, escape velocity, or unbound probability is calculated. All high-velocity objects are treated as ordinary runaway stars within the Galaxy. Therefore, no candidates meet the Stella inclusion criteria.

candidate_groups_considered.description: 37 runaway stars identified from LAMOST DR12 CBe stars with total peculiar velocities ~40–101 km/s.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper applies the classical runaway-star criterion (total peculiar velocity > 40 km/s) and labels these objects as runaway stars, but it does not discuss any of them as possibly unbound from the Galaxy. No escape-velocity or bound/unbound assessment is performed. They are treated as ordinary Galactic runaways.

source_refs.context: paper discussion of runaway star identification and peculiar velocity calculation

candidate_groups_considered.description: 13 newly identified CBe star binaries from LAMOST DR12.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: These objects are presented as spectroscopic binaries with radial-velocity variations. The paper does not discuss their Galactic-bound status or treat them as possible unbound/HVS candidates.

source_refs.context: paper discussion of CBe star binaries identification

candidate_groups_considered.description: 60 potential CBe star binaries flagged by high RUWE.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: These sources are proposed as potential wide binaries based on elevated Gaia RUWE values. The paper does not discuss their kinematics in terms of Galactic escape or unbound status.

source_refs.context: paper discussion of potential CBe star binaries via RUWE

candidate_groups_considered.description: 34 potential cluster members of CBe stars from LAMOST DR12.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: These objects are discussed as potential open-cluster members based on spatial, proper-motion, and distance consistency. They are not presented as high-velocity or unbound candidates.

source_refs.context: paper discussion of potential cluster member identification
